rulings.Case Title: GEOFFREY LYNCH VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 26, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT JUSTIN LUU, XIAO WU DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to July 9, 2024 per the order of the presiding judge. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. WILLIAM DAUGHERTY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To: 1) Compel Responses By Defendant To Demand For Indentification And Production Of Documents, Set One, And Production Without Objection; 2) Compel Responses By Defendant To First Set Of Special Interrogatories Without Objection;
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 26, 2024 line 2. PLAINTIFF TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION MOTION / NOTICE OF MOTION & MOTION TO: 1) COMPEL RESPONSES BY DEFENDANT TO DEMAND FOR INDENTIFICATION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, AND PRODUCTION WITHOUT OBJECTION; 2) COMPEL RESPONSES BY DEFENDANT TO FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES WITHOUT OBJECTION; is DENIED. Moving party failed to show by admissible evidence that discovery at issue had been served. While the declaration of Dylan Tong references Ex. E there is no such exhibit attached to the declaration. Exs. A-D are copies of the discovery purportedly propounded and do not include proofs of service. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GRANADA ENTERPRISE MANAGEMENT, LLC DBA THE VS. THOMAS TENG
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 26, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT THOMAS TENG DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Demurrer is overruled. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. Motion to strike the verification is granted for Plaintiff to verify the operative complaint in accordance with CCP 446. Motion to strike is otherwise denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
